tool.name: Proseg
tool.description: probabilistic cell segmentation for spatial transcriptomics
tool.command:
## Link the input file ln -s '$transcripts' transcripts.csv &&
proseg
## ── PLATFORM PRESET ── #if $platform.preset == "xenium": --xenium #elif $platform.preset == "cosmx": --cosmx #elif $platform.preset == "cosmx_micron": --cosmx-micron #elif $platform.preset == "merscope": --merscope #elif $platform.preset == "manual": --x-column '${platform.x_col}' --y-column '${platform.y_col}' --z-column '${platform.z_col}' --gene-column '${platform.gene_col}' #end if
## ── PRIOR SEGMENTATION ── #if $cellpose_masks: --cellpose-masks '$cellpose_masks' #end if
## ── MORPHOLOGY PRESET ── #if $tissue_preset.mode == "annular": --cell-compactness 0.02 --enforce-connectivity #elif $tissue_preset.mode == "custom": --cell-compactness $tissue_preset.cell_compactness $tissue_preset.enforce_connectivity #else: --cell-compactness 0.04 #end if
## ── VOXEL GEOMETRY AND SAMPLING ── --voxel-size $voxel_size --burnin-voxel-size $burnin_voxel_size --voxel-layers $voxel_layers --samples $samples --burnin-samples $burnin_samples
## ── OPTIONAL FLAGS ── $ignore_z #if $min_qv: --qv-column qv --min-qv $min_qv #end if
## ── ADVANCED OPTIONS ── --morphology-steps-per-iter $advanced.morph_steps --ncomponents $advanced.ncomponents --diffusion-probability $advanced.diffusion_prob
## ── POLYGON OUTPUT (optional) ── #if $generate_polygons: --output-cell-polygons '$output_polygons_file' #end if
## ── MAIN OUTPUT ── --output-spatialdata 'proseg-output.zarr'
transcripts.csv &&
## Copy zarr to Galaxy output path mkdir -p '$output_spatialdata.files_path' && cp -r proseg-output.zarr/* '$output_spatialdata.files_path/' && echo '{"proseg": "output"}' > '$output_spatialdata' &&
## Write run summary echo "ProSeg run complete." > '$run_summary'

param.name: transcripts
param.label: Transcript CSV
param.help: CSV file with one row per detected transcript. Must contain x, y, z coordinates and gene name. Produced by your spatial transcriptomics instrument (Xenium, MERSCOPE, or CosMx).

param.name: cellpose_masks
param.label: CellPose cell masks (.npy)
param.help: Prior segmentation required by ProSeg. Run CellPose on your DAPI image first and provide the resulting masks .npy file. ProSeg cannot detect cells from scratch — some form of prior is always needed.

param.name: preset
param.label: Platform preset
param.help: Select the preset matching your instrument. This sets the correct column names automatically. Use Manual if your CSV has non-standard column names.

param.name: mode
param.label: Morphology preset
param.help: Standard is appropriate for most tissue types. Annular is an experimental preset for cells with hollow or ring-shaped cytoplasm (e.g., steatotic hepatocytes, necrotic tumor cells). Custom allows manual parameter entry.

param.name: cell_compactness
param.label: Cell compactness
param.help: Controls how circular cells must be. Lower values allow non-circular (ring-shaped) cells. Default is 0.04. For steatotic tissue try values between 0.01 and 0.02.

param.name: enforce_connectivity
param.label: Enforce cell connectivity
param.help: Prevent cells from being assigned as two disconnected regions. Recommended for hollow or ring-shaped cells.

param.name: voxel_size
param.label: Voxel size (µm)
param.help: Physical size of each grid square in microns. Smaller values give finer boundary resolution but require more RAM and longer runtime. Default: 1.0 µm.

param.name: burnin_voxel_size
param.label: Burn-in voxel size (µm)
param.help: Coarser voxel size used during the burn-in phase. MUST be an exact integer multiple of voxel size. Example: if voxel size = 0.5, valid values are 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0. Default: 2.0 µm.

param.name: voxel_layers
param.label: Voxel layers (Z-axis)
param.help: Number of Z-axis voxel layers. Default 4 is appropriate for most tissue sections. Use the 2D option below for strictly flat data.

param.name: samples
param.label: Sampling iterations
param.help: Number of fine-grained sampling iterations after burn-in. More iterations give more stable results but take longer. Default: 200. For publication quality consider 400.

param.name: burnin_samples
param.label: Burn-in iterations
param.help: Number of rough warm-up iterations on the coarse grid before fine sampling begins. Default: 200.

param.name: ignore_z
param.label: Treat data as 2D (ignore Z coordinates)
param.help: Use for flat tissue sections or 2D synthetic data. Significantly reduces compute time.

param.name: min_qv
param.label: Minimum quality value (QV)
param.help: Filter out transcripts with quality scores below this threshold. Xenium standard is 20. Leave blank to skip filtering.

param.name: generate_polygons
param.label: Output cell boundary polygons
param.help: Write 2D cell boundary polygons as a parquet file. Useful for visualization in Napari or geopandas.

param.name: morph_steps
param.label: Morphology steps per iteration
param.help: Number of boundary pixel-flip proposals per sampling iteration. Increase to 8000 for complex shapes such as thin cytoplasmic rings. Proportionally increases runtime.

param.name: ncomponents
param.label: Expression model components
param.help: Number of latent cell-type components in the gene expression mixture model. Default: 10.

param.name: diffusion_prob
param.label: Transcript diffusion probability
param.help: Fraction of transcripts modelled as ambient diffused RNA. Default: 0.2.

tool.help:
**ProSeg** — Probabilistic Cell Segmentation for Spatial Transcriptomics
ProSeg uses a Cellular Potts Model (CPM) to refine initial cell boundaries using the spatial distribution of transcripts. It requires a prior segmentation (e.g., from CellPose on a DAPI image) to determine the number and approximate location of cells. ProSeg cannot detect cells from scratch.
-----
**Workflow**
1. Run CellPose on your DAPI image to produce ``cellpose_masks.npy`` 2. Provide the transcript CSV and masks file to this tool 3. Select the platform preset matching your instrument 4. Load the output zarr in Python::
import spatialdata as sd sdata = sd.read_zarr("proseg-output.zarr") adata = sdata.tables["table"]
-----
**Platform presets**
- **10x Xenium**: uses x_location, y_location, z_location, feature_name - **Vizgen MERSCOPE**: uses global_x, global_y, global_z, gene - **NanoString CosMx (pixel)**: pixel coordinates, converted to microns internally - **NanoString CosMx (micron)**: coordinates already in microns - **Manual**: specify column names from your CSV header via dropdown
-----
**Key parameters**
- **Cell compactness** (default 0.04): lower values allow non-circular cell shapes. Relevant for steatotic hepatocytes or necrotic tumor cells where cytoplasm forms a thin ring around a lipid or necrotic core.
- **Voxel size**: spatial resolution in microns. Smaller values give finer boundary resolution but require more RAM. The burn-in voxel size must be an exact integer multiple of the final voxel size.
- **Enforce connectivity**: prevents a single cell from being assigned as two disconnected regions. Recommended for hollow or ring-shaped cells.
- **Annular preset**: experimental preset for steatotic or hollow cells. Sets reduced compactness and enables connectivity enforcement.
-----
**Outputs**
- **SpatialData zarr**: the main output. Load with ``spatialdata.read_zarr()`` - **Cell boundary polygons** (optional): parquet file for visualization in Napari or geopandas - **Run summary**: plain text confirmation of successful completion
-----
**Quality check after running**
Load transcript metadata and check the unassigned rate::
import pandas as pd tx = pd.read_parquet("transcript_metadata.parquet") print(f"Unassigned: {(tx.cell_id == 0).mean():.1%}")
A rate above 30% suggests the prior segmentation needs improvement or the tissue morphology requires the Annular preset.
-----
**Citation**
Moses et al. (2023) doi:10.1101/2023.11.20.567950
